generatedFrom: repository source, specs, decision log (D-001..D-038), and commit history
note: Machine-readable metadata for the Deepak Labs project. Status is reported honestly; unknown external values are null rather than invented.
tagline: A Personal Operating System — the canonical, machine-readable record of a researcher-engineer's work, built to last a decade.
summary: Deepak Labs is a long-lived software product (not a portfolio template) that holds the authoritative record of one researcher-engineer's projects, publications, posts, timeline, and skills as a single interconnected graph, explorable by both humans and an AI assistant. It is built documentation-first, with every architectural decision recorded as an ADR so any future human or AI can resume the work from the repository alone. The shipped foundation is a Next.js 15 / React 19 application with a production-grade, tier-gated 3D hero runtime engineered as a pure enhancement layer; the backend (a modular monolith on PostgreSQL with a RAG-grounded AI assistant) is fully architected and awaiting implementation.
problem: A researcher-engineer's professional record is fragmented across rented platforms (LinkedIn, GitHub, Scholar, files), each telling a partial, platform-shaped story subject to algorithm and format changes. There is no canonical source; generic portfolios cannot express a dual researcher-engineer identity; the record is illegible to the AI systems that increasingly mediate discovery; and static portfolios decay because editing them is a project.
solution: One owned, permanent system that is canonical (external profiles become mirrors), alive (every surface updatable in minutes; freshness is a first-class public property), and machine-readable by default (the same structured content serves the human reader, the search index, and a corpus-grounded, citing AI assistant named 'Dex'). Content is modeled as a graph of typed relations rather than isolated pages. Every decision is shaped by one constraint: one maintainer, one decade, one personal budget.

architecture.shape: Modular monolith with a two-plane runtime kernel (Event Bus + Capability Registry) coordinating ten runtimes
architecture.ai: RAG grounded strictly in the corpus, three-layer domain restriction, source-cited answers
architecture.rendering: Static/SSG with incremental revalidation, CDN-first, SSR fallback
architecture.dataModel: Content graph: shared content spine + typed, closed relation set (implements, writes-about, produced, evidences, depicts, references)
architecture.heroRuntime: 3D scene as a pure enhancement layer; tier gate before any 3D byte; error ladder resolves every failure to a designed lesser tier
governingPrinciples: Fewest moving parts that satisfy the requirements
Decide expensive-to-reverse things early, defer cheap-to-reverse ones
The floor principle: 'the ceiling disappears, the room remains'
Machine-readable and human-readable are the same artifact

engineeringChallenges: Shipping a heavy 3D feature without regressing the core page: a tier gate that decides before any 3D byte downloads, verified by three.js being absent from every route's First Load JS.
60fps in React without re-render storms: continuous values (scroll, pointer, camera) held in mutable refs read in useFrame, never React state; discrete state in Zustand; content as server props.
Rendering a data-driven knowledge graph in 2 draw calls via a single InstancedMesh and one merged edge geometry.

Designing failure as a feature: an error ladder where WebGL context loss, GLB parse failure, chunk-load failure, and thermal collapse each resolve to a lesser tier that was already a designed experience.

Reconciling a mandated 'event-bus-only' directive with the reality that queries are request/response: a two-plane kernel (bus + capability registry) that satisfies decoupling without correlation-ID plumbing.
Enforcing absolute AI domain restriction with retrieval gating that declines before the LLM is called (reliability and cost control in one guard).

Absorbing a late, high-risk owner direction change (2D -> stylized 3D hero) without abandoning principles: surface the conflict, record the risks, lift the ban narrowly, design fully before any asset spend.

research.orientation: Researcher-engineer dual identity is the product's central differentiator; publications carry a plainSummary field as the researcher-to-engineer bridge.
appliedResearchInProduct: Applied RAG system design with strict grounding and citation
Machine-legible personal knowledge graph as agent-era infrastructure
research.publicationsNote: No publications recorded in the repository yet; the publication content type and academic-metadata model (authors, venue, year, abstract, bibtex, pdfUrl) are implemented and awaiting real records.

metrics.metricsNote: These measure engineering discipline and shipped foundation quality, not product traffic — there is none yet by design (not publicly launched).

githubUrlNote: No public repository URL recorded in the project (outbound.github is null in content/site.ts).
demoNote: Not publicly deployed. The 3D hero foundation runs on a dev-only route (/dev/hero) that 404s in production. Public release is gated on real content and copy-testing protocols.

screenshots.note: See SCREENSHOTS.md for full shot list and capture guidance

milestones.label: Hero runtime foundation shipped (Sprint H-01) + Personal OS runtime architecture

roadmap.label: Canonical record: Landing, About, Projects, Publications, Posts, Timeline, Skills, Contact, GitHub display, core Admin CMS, SEO/RSS/search
AI assistant (Dex) + knowledge-base admin, Gallery, deep GitHub, full analytics

statusDetail: Architecture-complete and foundation-shipped; backend, CMS, AI assistant, and 3D Twin assets are designed but not yet built. Not publicly launched.

difficulty.rationale: High for the breadth of concerns held coherently by one maintainer: a full-stack product architecture, a design system with enforced laws, a tier-gated WebGL runtime with hard performance budgets, an applied RAG design, and a runtime-kernel abstraction — all documented to ADR standard. Not a 9-10 because the hardest backend and AI pieces are architected rather than implemented, and traffic-scale problems are explicitly out of scope.

innovationScore.rationale: The novelty is not a new algorithm but a rare synthesis: treating a personal site as a decade-horizon, machine-readable operating system; documentation-first development with an AI-continuity memory system; a 3D hero engineered as a strictly non-blocking enhancement layer with an error ladder; and a two-plane kernel that consciously overrides a naive 'event-bus-only' brief with a reasoned request/response plane. The engineering judgment — especially what is deferred and why — is the innovation.
distinctiveElements: Freshness as a first-class public property
Tier-gated 3D with 'the ceiling disappears, the room remains'

futureWork: Supply reference photos to unblock the Twin style gate (#1); commission style frames in the 'Drafted Laboratory' direction.

Populate real content (projects, publications, posts) and pass the R4 identity-sentence release protocol.
Build the admin CMS with the write-once-surface-everywhere publish flow.
Ship the Dex AI assistant (v1.5): register IDexSession + IRetrieval; the shipped hero body and navigation hand-off already listen (no rework seam).
Ratify deployment vendors (docs/10), run the first production Lighthouse audit as release evidence, and deploy Tier 0 before the 3D scene exists.
Build the Knowledge Map as a view over the existing graph data (a v2+ knowledge-graph navigation surface).
Expose a public read-only programmatic/agent API over the read layer (the machine-readable principle realized).
